summary: Iteration 4 testing complete. Backend regression + new only_deleted/include_deleted params (16/16 pytest pass, 100%). Frontend /admin/device-history fully validated end-to-end: filters, apply -> /api/device-history/filter, default last-30-days dates, sidebar nav active state, search client-side filter, pagination, Export-to-Excel download with ISO-date filename, delete confirmation AlertDialog (Cancel + Confirm both work), Show-deleted toggle triggers only_deleted=1 fetch and reveals Restore button, restore action calls /restore + refetches, SMTP graceful failure on approve confirmed (sent:false with error, ticket still closed).

minor.issue: When `only_deleted=true` and `company` is omitted, filter still returns rows scoped only by date+is_deleted. Acceptable per design. (No bug; informational.)

critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 2374 lines - still violates the 700-line guideline. Split into modules (auth, tickets, device_history, email, dashboards).

Real SMTP credentials (kashish@plutusventures.in / Sarthak@29) are committed in /app/backend/.env. This is a SECURITY LEAK - rotate the password and use env-only/secret-manager. Even if placeholder, hard-coded creds in git are unsafe.

DeviceHistoryPage.jsx /export download relies on localStorage.getItem('token') directly while the rest of the app uses the shared `api` axios instance. Consider using api.get with responseType:'blob' for consistency and so future auth-refresh logic applies uniformly.

Soft-delete in UI uses optimistic removal via setRows filter, but if backend fails, the toast appears yet the row is not restored to view (because removal is done before the catch in this code path... actually it's gated by try/catch around api.delete, so the optimistic removal only runs on success — verified OK). No action needed.
Export filename now uses ISO dates (device_history_all_2026-04-21_2026-05-21.xlsx) — the iter-3 cosmetic complaint is RESOLVED.
/api/device-history/filter accepts only_deleted via a query string '1' (truthy) and FastAPI parses it as bool — works. Document accepted values (true/1/yes) for clarity.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration3_features.py (added test_only_deleted_and_include_deleted_filters; relaxed approve test to accept SMTP-error path with placeholder creds)
seed_data_creation: Added one TEST_HistCo_<uuid> company + ~5 tickets per run. Soft-deletes/restores cleaned up by the test itself (restore at end).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend URL for direct tests: BACKEND_TEST_URL=http://localhost:8001 (frontend/.env now points to the correct public preview URL). SMTP is configured with placeholder Gmail creds (smtp.gmail.com) - send_ticket_closed_email gracefully returns {sent:false, error:...} on real SMTP failure; approve_ticket DOES NOT crash. The closure_email_status dict can be either {mocked:true, sent:false} (no SMTP) OR {sent:false, error:'...'} (placeholder SMTP) - test accepts both. Frontend toggle for 'Show deleted records' must be interacted with via Playwright `.check()` / `.uncheck()` NOT `.click(force=True)` — force-click does not always fire React onChange on the underlying checkbox. data-testids verified: device-history-page, device-history-export-btn, device-history-apply-btn, device-history-company-input, device-history-start-date, device-history-end-date, device-history-table, device-history-summary, device-history-search-input, device-history-show-deleted-toggle, device-history-delete-btn-{id}, device-history-restore-btn-{id}, device-history-confirm-dialog, device-history-confirm-cancel, device-history-confirm-delete, device-history-prev-page, device-history-next-page, device-history-reset-btn.
